name: Ermita de San Baudelio – round trip from Casillas de Berlanga
name_original: Ermita de San Baudelio – ida y vuelta desde Casillas de Berlanga
region_intro: Hiking around Caltojar offers a variety of trails exploring the region's diverse landscape. The area features river canyons, historical sites, and viewpoints, providing a mix of natural and cultural interest. Terrain includes paths along river valleys and routes leading to elevated miradors, with gentle to moderate elevation changes. The trails are suitable for different fitness levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. Best hiking trails around Caltojar The most popular hiking route is Talegones River Canyon Trail, an 8.5 mile (13.7 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Talegones River, offering scenic views of the canyon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador Portillo de Andaluz y Refugio Palomar Risco – circular desde Andaluz, a moderate 7.8 mile (12.6 km) path. This circular trail includes a viewpoint and a refuge, showcasing the local landscape. Local hikers also love the Ermita de San Baudelio – round trip from Casillas de Berlanga, a 2.1 mile (3.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes to a historic hermitage, often completed in about 56 minutes. Hiking around Caltojar is defined by river valleys, historical landmarks, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for both easy walks and moderate excursions. The routes in Caltojar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a review. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Caltojar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Caltojar?
A: Yes, Caltojar offers several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059254497/ermita-de-san-baudelio-round-trip-from-casillas-de-berlanga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Baudelio – round trip from Casillas de Berlanga&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.1-mile trail leading to a historic hermitage, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berlanga Castle – Berlanga de Duero loop from Berlanga de Duero&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.1-mile loop around the castle.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Caltojar?
A: Hiking trails in Caltojar vary in length and duration. You can find shorter walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059254497/ermita-de-san-baudelio-round-trip-from-casillas-de-berlanga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Baudelio – round trip from Casillas de Berlanga&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 2.1 miles and takes less than an hour. Longer, moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062321130/talegones-river-canyon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talegones River Canyon Trail&lt;/a&gt; span about 8.5 miles and typically require around 3.5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Caltojar area?
A: Yes, Caltojar features several circular routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063946620/mirador-portillo-de-andaluz-y-refugio-palomar-risco-circular-desde-andaluz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Portillo de Andaluz y Refugio Palomar Risco – circular desde Andaluz&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.8-mile path that includes a viewpoint and a refuge. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263525850/fuentegelmes-torete-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentegelmes - Torete River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 6.7 miles.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Caltojar?
A: The Caltojar region is characterized by diverse natural landscapes, including scenic river canyons, such as those along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062321130/talegones-river-canyon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talegones River Canyon Trail&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter elevated miradors offering panoramic views, and routes that traverse rural landscapes.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can be explored near Caltojar's hiking trails?
A: Caltojar's hiking trails often lead to or pass by significant historical sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berlanga Castle&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2701891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Berlanga de Duero&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2677132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rello Walled Village&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059254497/ermita-de-san-baudelio-round-trip-from-casillas-de-berlanga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Baudelio&lt;/a&gt; is another notable historical hermitage accessible by trail.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Caltojar?
A: The hiking routes in Caltojar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, which includes river valleys, historical landmarks, and scenic viewpoints, offering a mix of natural beauty and cultural interest.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails in Caltojar?
A: Yes, the region is known for its scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063946620/mirador-portillo-de-andaluz-y-refugio-palomar-risco-circular-desde-andaluz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Portillo de Andaluz&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent viewpoint included in one of the circular trails. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5587359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andaluz Geodetic Vertex (1,027 m)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers elevated perspectives of the landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Caltojar?
A: While specific seasonal details aren't provided, the varied terrain of river valleys and elevated miradors suggests that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer might be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter could offer a different, quieter experience, though some higher routes might be more challenging.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological features to see?
A: Yes, the area around Caltojar features interesting natural monuments. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5616955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Utrillas Formations, La Riba de Escalote&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7161140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocky Cliff in the Paramera&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7161103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Ojo&lt;/a&gt; (Cave of the Eye).

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Caltojar region?
A: There are at least 5 distinct hiking trails documented for the Caltojar region, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to moderate excursions through river valleys and historical landscapes.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or offer waterside views?
A: Yes, the region is known for its river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062321130/talegones-river-canyon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talegones River Canyon Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, following the Talegones River and offering scenic views of the canyon. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263525850/fuentegelmes-torete-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentegelmes - Torete River Loop&lt;/a&gt; also features a river component.
tips:
The hermitage of San Baudelio is located in an isolated spot two kilometers southeast of the small Soria town of Casillas de Berlanga, settling on a gentle slope that descends towards the course of the Escalote River.

The hermitage was part of a small monastery to which the documentary sources do not attribute a long survival. Over time it became the private property of several residents of Casillas.

The exterior appearance of the building does not announce the exceptionality of the singularity, originality and amazing architectural and pictorial marvels that the small hermitage of San Baudelio treasures (in this case the verb and the adjectives are appropriate).

Its assignment is, in the opinion of the researchers, clearly pre-Romanesque and with clear Mozarabic influences.

This hermitage is considered the most original jewel of Soria and Hispanic pre-Romanesque architecture. Traditional historiography has been defining the hermitage of San Baudelio de Casillas de Berlanga as "The Sistine Chapel of Mozarabic art".
